title: Stellaria capillipes
taxonomy: Stellaria capillipes is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This plant is further classified in the genus Stellaria, which includes about 120 species of plants known as starworts. The genus Stellaria is part of the order Caryophyllales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Within the family Caryophyllaceae, Stellaria capillipes is closely related to other species such as Stellaria alsine and Stellaria graminea. The classification of Stellaria capillipes is an important tool for understanding its evolutionary relationships and identifying the plant accurately.
uses: Stellaria capillipes, a plant native to the Himalayas and parts of China, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves, stems, and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for infections and wounds. In traditional Chinese medicine, Stellaria capillipes is used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Stellaria capillipes for medicinal purposes.

intro: Stellaria capillipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the Chinese starwort. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stellaria capillipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Stellaria capillipes is often processed into teas, infusions, and tablets, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulas to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
